purpose: Institutional partners (colleges, coaching centers, universities) that refer student cohorts to the marketing website. Stores partnership metadata, contact info, and referral codes for attribution.

important_fields.name: name
important_fields.note: Official name of the college or institution (e.g., 'IIT Delhi', 'ABC Coaching Center').

important_fields.name: contact_email
important_fields.note: Email of the primary contact person (college administrator, coordinator, or educator responsible for institutional partnerships).

important_fields.name: referral_code
important_fields.note: Unique code or identifier used in referral links (e.g., 'college_iitdelhi_2026'). Enables tracking which college referred a signup via signup_sources.

important_fields.name: institution_type
important_fields.note: Type of institution for segmentation and messaging. Helps tailor outreach and feature requests.

important_fields.name: status
important_fields.note: Partnership status: 'prospecting' (negotiating), 'active' (live trial/partnership), or 'inactive' (ended). Filters for current partnerships in analytics.

important_fields.name: updated_at
important_fields.type: TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
important_fields.note: Last update (status change, contact update, etc.).

relationships.note: Colleges are referenced via signup_sources.source_identifier when source_type is 'college_referral'. No direct foreign key; lookup by referral_code or college_id via signup_sources.

notes: This table is separate from signups because colleges may have multiple attributes (contact info, institution type, partnership status) that are used for reporting and outreach, independent of individual signups. When a student signs up via a college referral link, the signup is recorded with a source_identifier pointing to this college's referral_code. This enables bulk operations (e.g., 'notify all educators at colleges with active partnerships') and college-specific analytics ('how many students from XYZ College signed up this month?').
